body.question_1: How would you rate the overall quality of teaching in your courses this semester so far?
body.answer_1: The teaching quality in my Digital Communication courses at the School of Communication has been exceptional this semester. My lecturers consistently demonstrate deep subject matter expertise and present complex material in a clear, well-structured, and engaging manner that truly enhances comprehension. I particularly value the integration of real-world industry examples and hands-on exercises into every session, which makes abstract theoretical concepts genuinely applicable. The interactive teaching style — characterized by open discussions, collaborative problem-solving, and prompt feedback on assignments — has significantly accelerated my academic growth. The faculty's visible dedication and responsiveness to students makes me confident I am receiving a high-calibre education that will serve me well in my future career.

body.question_2: How well does the course content align with current industry standards and your career aspirations?
body.answer_2: The Digital Communication curriculum at the School of Communication covers the essential theoretical foundations competently and provides a reasonable structure for academic progression throughout the degree. Core modules are acceptable in their depth and breadth, covering key concepts that form the basis of professional practice in this field. However, the elective offerings could be significantly expanded to reflect emerging trends and specialized career pathways that students are increasingly interested in. Greater integration of industry collaboration — such as guest lectures, live projects, and sponsored capstone work — would substantially strengthen the curriculum's practical relevance and better prepare graduates for competitive employment.

body.question_3: How effective are the assessment methods (assignments, quizzes, exams) in evaluating your understanding of the subject matter?
body.answer_3: The assessment methods employed in my Digital Communication courses at the School of Communication are commendable and demonstrate a thoughtful approach to measuring genuine student understanding. The balanced mix of individual assignments, group projects, presentations, and written examinations ensures that different learning styles and competencies are recognized and fairly evaluated throughout the semester. Rubrics are consistently shared in advance, assessment criteria are transparent, and feedback is provided promptly enough to inform improvement in subsequent tasks. I particularly acknowledge the emphasis on applied problem-solving assessments rather than rote memorization, which I believe is a much stronger indicator of actual competency and readiness for professional practice.

body.question_4: How would you describe the availability and responsiveness of your lecturers outside of scheduled class time?
body.answer_4: Lecturer availability at the School of Communication for my Digital Communication courses is acceptable, with noticeable differences between individual faculty members in their accessibility and responsiveness to student needs outside of scheduled class sessions. A number of lecturers are readily available and proactive in offering support, which I find invaluable during intensive assignment and examination periods. Others, however, have limited and poorly communicated office hours, making it challenging to get timely guidance on coursework challenges. Establishing a standardized minimum availability requirement and publishing these schedules on the student portal would greatly improve accessibility for all students.

body.question_5: How satisfied are you with the learning resources and materials provided for your courses this semester?
body.answer_5: The learning materials provided for several of my Digital Communication courses at the School of Communication are inconsistent and fall short of what students need to succeed academically. Required textbooks are outdated and frequently out of stock in both the campus bookstore and the library, forcing students to source materials independently at considerable expense and inconvenience. The Learning Management System is inconsistently used by lecturers — some post materials days after class, and some modules have no digital resources whatsoever. I strongly raise concerns about the Library Services and IT Support Department to collaborate on ensuring all course resources are accessible digitally, current, and available before the semester commences.

body.question_6: How would you rate the IT infrastructure and digital learning tools provided by the university to support your studies?
body.answer_6: The IT infrastructure at the School of Communication, maintained by the IT Support Department, performs at a reasonable level that meets basic student needs but falls short of what is required to fully support a modern, technology-dependent academic environment. Wi-Fi connectivity is generally stable in primary teaching spaces but becomes inconsistent during peak usage hours and in peripheral campus locations. Software availability in computer labs is adequate for core coursework but limited for students requiring specialized applications for advanced projects. Expanding bandwidth capacity, updating hardware on a regular replacement cycle, and improving helpdesk responsiveness would meaningfully elevate the digital learning experience for the entire student community.

body.question_7: How efficient have you found the academic administrative processes such as registration, grade appeals, and scheduling?
body.answer_7: Academic administrative processes at the School of Communication are moderate in their execution, meeting basic requirements but leaving clear room for improvement in both speed and user-friendliness. The online student portal functions adequately for routine tasks such as registration and fee payment, but navigating more complex requests — such as grade appeals or course exemptions — remains unnecessarily cumbersome. Communication timelines are inconsistent, with some requests handled promptly and others taking weeks without updates or acknowledgment. Streamlining administrative workflows, improving the student portal's UX, and setting clear service-level response standards would meaningfully reduce administrative friction for the entire student body.

body.question_8: How would you evaluate the quality and accessibility of student support services currently available at this university?
body.answer_8: Student support services at the School of Communication, managed by the Student Affairs Office, are insufficient and fall significantly short of what students need to manage the academic, personal, and financial challenges of university life. Mental health support in particular is severely limited — appointment wait times can exceed three weeks, which is unacceptable for students experiencing acute stress. Financial hardship assistance processes are slow, bureaucratic, and poorly communicated, leaving vulnerable students without timely help during critical periods. The university must urgently expand its counseling capacity, simplify hardship application procedures, and raise awareness of available services through more proactive and visible outreach to the student community.

body.question_9: How well does the university's physical environment and facilities support your daily learning experience?
body.answer_9: The physical facilities at the School of Communication, under the responsibility of the Facilities Management, present concerning conditions that negatively impact the daily learning experience of students. Several lecture halls have malfunctioning air conditioning systems that make studying in warm months genuinely uncomfortable and distracting. Toilet facilities in older buildings are poorly maintained and frequently reported without timely resolution from the facilities team. Study spaces are chronically insufficient relative to the student population, particularly during examination periods when demand peaks significantly. I urge the university to prioritize facilities maintenance budgets, establish a transparent issue-reporting system, and expand study space capacity as part of its next capital improvement plan.

body.question_10: What specific improvements would you most like to see implemented for the remainder of this semester?
body.answer_10: My main recommendations for improving the Digital Communication student experience at the School of Communication center on consistency, communication, and investment in areas that currently deliver only reasonable outcomes. First, standardizing teaching quality, assessment practices, and resource provision across all modules would significantly reduce the frustration caused by the wide variability students currently experience between courses. Second, improving the speed and transparency of administrative and financial communications would reduce unnecessary anxiety and wasted time for students. Finally, expanding industry engagement through internship pipelines, career workshops, and alumni networking events would better equip graduates for the increasingly competitive job market they will enter upon graduation.
